title: Spring and Fall: to a young child
content:
آ آ آ آ Mï؟½rgarï؟½t, ï؟½re you grï؟½eving
آ آ آ آ Over Goldengrove unleaving?
آ آ آ آ Leï؟½ves, like the things of man, you
آ آ آ آ With your fresh thoughts care for, can you?
آ آ آ آ ï؟½h! ï؟½s the heart grows older
آ آ آ آ It will come to such sights colder
آ آ آ آ By and by, nor spare a sigh
آ آ آ آ Though worlds of wanwood leafmeal lie;
آ آ آ آ And yet you wï؟½ll weep and know why.
آ آ آ آ Now no matter, child, the name:
آ آ آ آ Sï؟½rrow's sprï؟½ngs ï؟½re the same.
آ آ آ آ Nor mouth had, no nor mind, expressed
آ آ آ آ What heart heard of, ghost guessed:
آ آ آ آ It is the blight man was born for,
آ آ آ آ It is Margaret you mourn for.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Gerard Manley Hopkins delivers a powerful performance in "Spring and Fall: to a young child"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
